content.passage: Because he is very protective, Ralph stepped in to prevent his brother, who was only 8 years old, ________ fighting the older students.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

content.passage: After stepping onto the porch, the entire family was ________ seeing a bear in the backyard.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

content.passage: The treatment was administered and then patients were asked to return the next day since the ________ of the treatment, if there were any, do not appear for 24 hours.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

solution.explanation: 'Effects' (noun, plural) meaning results. A/B are verbs, C singular.

content.passage: Before the story about the financials became public, Aura System, an electric battery company, ________ gone public, but now there is no chance of that happening.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

solution.explanation: Correct modal: 'might have' for past possibility. A/B misuse 'of' for 'have.'

content.passage: Research performed on the hummingbird population indicated that more time spent searching for food ________ a longer lifespan.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

content.passage: The invention of barcodes made cashiers' jobs so much easier, as it substantially ________ of minutes that it took to calculate the total price of a customer's purchase.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

solution.explanation: 'Number of minutes' (countable). A/B use 'amount' for uncountable.

content.passage: After turning in my paper, my teacher reminded me that I needed to ________ all of my sources.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

solution.explanation: 'Cite' means reference sources. A 'sight' vision, B 'site' location.

content.passage: For Donations Unlimited, donated clothes that are in fair to good condition are much easier to resell ________ used household items, such as pans, cutlery, or rice cookers.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

content.passage: Selecting the right marketing team and publishing strategy directly ________ the success – or failure – of the book launch.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

content.passage: Clear glass windows were installed in the house ________ letting in more natural light.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

content.passage: Before finalizing her decision to buy, Sarah did ________ research on the property values in the neighborhood to make sure that buying the house was a good financial decision.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

content.passage: Some mycologists prefer studying fungi in a laboratory ________ others prefer to do field work and attempt to identify new species of mushrooms in the wild.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

content.passage: The five-star hotel lived up to its promise of excellent service: the staff were amazingly efficient, provided fantastic recommendations for the best restaurants and attractions in the city, and assisted in booking all of our transportations to _______ that we enjoyed our stay.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

solution.explanation: 'Ensure' means make certain. B 'assure' convince, C 'insure' protect financially.

content.passage: If I had a nickel for every time that I saw a dog ride a ________ broke long ago.
content.question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
options: A) bike, I would have been
C) bike, I would of been

solution.explanation: Conditional: 'would have been' (past perfect). C 'of' error.
